name: SingleTouch
comment:
This module exposes functions
to deal with single touch interactions.

The coordinates provided are the ones of the first touch in the
["changedTouches"](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/TouchEvent) list.
As a consequence, it may behave inconsistently
in case of an accidental multitouch usage.
In case of a need for consistency with potential
unwanted multitouch interactions,
you might want to use the `MultiTouch` module which provides
finer grained control over the processing of the touch event.

@docs onStart, onMove, onEnd, onCancel

name: MultiTouch
Touch

aliases.name: Coordinates
aliases.comment:
A simple type alias for the coordinates of a JavaScript
[Touch](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Touch) object.

aliases.name: Event
aliases.comment:
Type alias for a touch event.

The properties `touches`, `targetTouches` and `changedTouches`
are accessible through their corresponding functions.

To have more info about these properties and how to use them,
please refer to the [Mozilla documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Events/touchstart)

values.name: changedTouches
values.comment:
Retrieve the `changedTouches` property.

It returns a dictionary whose keys are the unique identifiers
of the touches.

values.name: clientPos
values.comment: Retrieve the clientX and clientY coordinates.

values.name: targetTouches
values.comment:
Retrieve the `targetTouches` property.

It returns a dictionary whose keys are the unique identifiers
of the touches.

values.name: touches
values.comment:
Retrieve the `touches` property.

It returns a dictionary whose keys are the unique identifiers
of the touches.
